mapping.a_element: the input point set (92M halo positions) from which structure is inferred
mapping.b_element: the record of projective local measurements along the observer's worldline

mapping.a_element: the simplicial complex (Delaunay tessellation) built over the points
mapping.b_element: the simplicial complex assembled from the recorded measurements

mapping.a_element: the recovered cosmic-web filament network / topology of large-scale structure
mapping.b_element: the topological information about (fluctuating) spacetime the complex is claimed to capture

brief_justification: The only real correspondence is the high-level, generic idea of building a simplicial complex from data to read off topology; the actual transferable machinery differs entirely. Paper A's method is a computational-geometry pipeline — Delaunay tessellation plus DisPerSE's discrete-Morse persistence, scaled by an overlapping-block decomposition with a circumsphere validity criterion and boundary stitching — with no analogue of Paper B's von Neumann algebra of observables, trace, or projection-valued measurements, and vice versa, so no role-for-role machinery mapping (thresholds, governing construction, generating rule) holds.
